author: cperezz.eth
content:
I think the VOPS-AA part is OK. Only as long as we are sure that whatever standard for programmable wallets that gets adopted won't need ARBITRARY state.

If we can confidently say that with top 10 or 20 slots of the account storage we have enough to cover all major use cases. I think this is shippable.

Referring to my article on that JIC: https://ethresear.ch/t/frame-transactions-through-a-statelessness-lens/24538


The biggest risk is we ship with mempool rules that are too strict, another standard gets adopted. And then, no AA tx has any mempool inclusion guarantees.

How many account storage slots do we need to have to support <@316280621783580673> 's use cases? Also, is there any widely adopted case that requires reading arbitrary state??
